btc-pool-contracts.title: BTC Pool Contracts
btc-pool-contracts.subtitle: Detailed overview of SHA-256 strategy logic, daily accruals, and contract operations.
btc-pool-contracts.intro: BTC Pool Contracts are designed for users who want managed Bitcoin mining exposure without running hardware on their own. The contract follows a daily accrual model so users can track forecast and realized dynamics directly in the dashboard.

blocks.heading: How the strategy operates
blocks.text: After activation, the contract is linked to SHA-256 pool infrastructure. Performance is modeled with market price inputs, network difficulty trends, and platform operating coefficients.

blocks.heading: What drives outcomes
blocks.text: Final economics depend on BTC volatility, difficulty changes, infrastructure uptime, and service costs. The interface therefore uses conservative gross-to-net reporting rather than fixed-profit promises.

blocks.heading: Dashboard visibility
blocks.text: Users can monitor contract status, daily accrual rows, cumulative net result, and breakeven projection. This supports decision-making based on real trajectory, not only entry assumptions.

points: Users seeking structured and predictable exposure to BTC mining strategy.
Users who want daily accrual visibility in a single account workflow.
Users who prefer conservative planning over aggressive ROI claims.
btc-pool-contracts.outro: Important: BTC Pool Contracts remain market-sensitive. Final performance depends on live network and market conditions.

security-storage.title: Security Storage
security-storage.subtitle: How cold-reserve controls, access policies, and audit trails support platform integrity.
security-storage.intro: Security Storage represents the operational custody layer for platform reserves. It is not just a wallet endpoint, but a structured control system covering access boundaries, sensitive actions, and event traceability.

blocks.heading: Custody architecture
blocks.text: Reserve funds are handled in an isolated operational perimeter with restricted access paths. Critical operations are role-separated to reduce single-point failure risk and unauthorized manipulation.

blocks.heading: Operational safeguards
blocks.text: Sensitive actions are governed by role-scoped permissions, approval routines, and exception handling policies. Non-standard operations require additional review and are documented for accountability.

blocks.heading: Auditability and response
blocks.text: Security-relevant events are recorded in audit trails: actor, action, timing, and outcome. This improves traceability and speeds up incident response when anomalies appear.

points: Users prioritizing operational security posture over purely marketing metrics.
Teams that require verifiable action history and governance controls.
Users evaluating platform reliability through risk and control discipline.
security-storage.outro: Security Storage strengthens trust through a process-first model: controlled access, documented actions, and governed escalation.

expert-support.title: Expert Support
expert-support.subtitle: How 24/7 support works: triage, SLA routing, escalation, and case closure.
expert-support.intro: Expert Support is the service layer for user requests across contracts, accruals, top-ups, and withdrawals. It operates 24/7 and follows structured triage, routing, and escalation policies to keep response quality stable.

blocks.heading: Initial ticket processing
blocks.text: Each request enters triage first: category, priority, and severity are defined before assignment. This helps route the case into the right workflow and improves first meaningful response speed.

blocks.heading: SLA and escalation model
blocks.text: Response targets vary by priority band. Complex or sensitive issues are escalated to operations/compliance while the user receives tracked progress updates through the ticket lifecycle.

blocks.heading: Communication transparency
blocks.text: Message history, status transitions, and case outcomes are logged in one thread. This prevents context loss and keeps communication auditable for both users and internal teams.

points: Users who expect fast operational response across dashboard actions.
Users who need status visibility from first contact to final closure.
Users who want one coordinated support channel for technical and process questions.
expert-support.outro: Expert Support improves service predictability from first message to documented case resolution.

aboutReadMore.title: CloudMine Infrastructure & Operating Model
aboutReadMore.subtitle: How we structure managed hashpower, risk controls, and transparent payout reporting in simulation mode.
aboutReadMore.intro: This page describes the operational logic behind our simulation environment: allocation of purchased capacity, conservative earnings assumptions, and transparent accounting for accruals and fees.
aboutReadMore.outro: Important: all profitability values are model outputs, not fixed-return commitments. Real market conditions, network difficulty, and fee dynamics can materially change final outcomes.

blocks.heading: Managed Hashpower Allocation
blocks.text: Contracts are mapped to strategy pools by algorithm profile (BTC SHA-256, LTC+DOGE Scrypt, KAS kHeavyHash). The allocation model targets stable daily accrual behavior while accounting for network condition shifts.

blocks.heading: Operational Risk Controls
blocks.text: The platform tracks strategy-level fee factors, uptime assumptions, and volatility bands. This helps users evaluate contract economics through conservative assumptions instead of fixed-return promises.

blocks.heading: Transparent Reporting Workflow
blocks.text: Users can monitor contract status, daily accrual rows, and aggregate mining performance inside one dashboard. Ledger-linked payout records are designed to keep deposits, purchases, accruals, and withdrawals auditable.

blocks.heading: Data Center & Power Assumptions
blocks.text: Our simulation baseline reflects common industry practices: geographically distributed facilities, power-cost sensitivity, and periodic efficiency updates. Instead of assuming perfect conditions, the model uses buffered uptime and operating ranges.

blocks.heading: Fee Logic & Cost Breakdown
blocks.text: Projected results include a layered cost structure inspired by public cloud-mining disclosures: infrastructure overhead, maintenance, and service operations. This allows users to compare gross vs net performance rather than seeing only headline returns.

blocks.heading: Monitoring, Alerts, and Audit Trail
blocks.text: Each key event (deposit conversion, contract activation, accrual, withdrawal state changes) is recorded in the platform ledger. This creates a traceable history for users and administrators and improves operational accountability.

blocks.heading: Payout Timing & Scenario Modeling
blocks.text: Expected payouts are shown as simulation intervals and may vary by strategy profile. The dashboard emphasizes scenario-based planning (base, conservative assumptions) to reduce over-optimistic forecasts.

blocks.heading: Support & Manual Operations
blocks.text: For edge cases, users can open support conversations in real time. Manual review flows (for example, compliance checks or withdrawal handling) are logged to maintain transparency between user actions and operator responses.

teamStory.title: How Our Team Runs Daily Operations
paragraphs: CloudMine runs as a cross-functional operating model where infrastructure, support, finance, and compliance work in one coordinated cycle.
Daily operations begin with a structured handoff: uptime, queue health, active alerts, and service readiness checkpoints.
Support and onboarding handle first response, route cases to owners, and keep communication traceable through the full lifecycle.
Compliance and security monitor policy triggers in parallel with operations to keep sensitive actions controlled and reviewable.
Financial operations reconcile deposits, accrual events, and withdrawal stages in a ledger-linked flow for transparent gross-to-net tracking.

amlPolicy.title: AML / CFT Policy
amlPolicy.intro: CloudMine applies a risk-based Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing framework designed for digital-asset services. This policy outlines customer due diligence, sanctions controls, transaction monitoring, and escalation procedures used on the platform.

sections.heading: 1. Scope and Compliance Commitment
sections.text: This policy applies to account onboarding, wallet operations, deposits, mining contract purchases, and withdrawals. CloudMine implements AML/CFT controls in line with generally accepted compliance standards and cooperates with lawful requests from competent authorities.

sections.heading: 2. Risk-Based Customer Due Diligence (CDD)
sections.text: Users may be asked to provide identity and contact information based on risk level, transaction profile, and jurisdictional exposure. Enhanced due diligence may apply for higher-risk patterns, including source-of-funds clarifications and additional verification steps.

sections.heading: 3. Sanctions, PEP, and Restricted Jurisdiction Screening
sections.text: CloudMine performs sanctions and politically exposed person (PEP) checks during onboarding and throughout the relationship lifecycle. Accounts linked to sanctioned persons, prohibited entities, or restricted geographies may be declined, restricted, or suspended.

sections.heading: 4. Transaction Monitoring and Behavioral Controls
sections.text: The platform monitors deposits, transfers, and withdrawal requests for unusual behavior, velocity anomalies, structuring signals, and other AML red flags. Automated and manual controls are used to detect activity that may require review or escalation.

sections.heading: 5. Suspicious Activity Escalation
sections.text: If suspicious behavior is detected, CloudMine may request additional documents, delay processing, freeze selected operations, or reject transactions pending compliance review. Relevant cases can be escalated for internal investigation and regulatory reporting where required.

sections.heading: 6. Recordkeeping and Audit Trail
sections.text: CloudMine keeps auditable records of key compliance events, including verification outcomes, wallet activity metadata, and case actions. Data retention periods are determined by legal obligations, security requirements, and incident response practices.

sections.heading: 7. Ongoing Program Governance
sections.text: AML/CFT controls are reviewed periodically and adjusted for emerging risks, operational changes, and legal updates. Team members involved in support and operations follow internal procedures for alert triage, case handoff, and decision logging.

sections.heading: 8. User Responsibilities
sections.text: Users must provide accurate information, avoid circumvention attempts, and refrain from using the platform for illegal purposes, including laundering, sanctions evasion, fraud, or terror financing. Violations may result in immediate account restrictions.

amlPolicy.outro: CloudMine does not guarantee uninterrupted access to all features during compliance reviews. Risk controls are applied to protect users, the platform, and the broader ecosystem.

sections.heading: 1. Scope of Service
sections.text: CloudMine provides a cloud-mining simulation platform where users can purchase strategy-based contracts, view accrual analytics, and manage account balances. Access to specific features may depend on account verification, regional availability, and operational controls.

sections.heading: 2. Risk Disclosure
sections.text: Mining economics are inherently volatile and depend on asset prices, network difficulty, fee levels, and uptime conditions. Any projected yield, APY, or profitability metric is an estimate and does not constitute a guaranteed return.

sections.heading: 3. Payments, Accruals, and Withdrawals
sections.text: Deposits may be converted to USDT-equivalent accounting balances according to available pricing sources at processing time. Contract accruals are recorded by platform logic and may vary by strategy and network parameters. Withdrawals are subject to security checks, compliance controls, and platform limits.

sections.heading: 4. User Responsibilities
sections.text: Users must provide accurate registration data, protect account credentials, and comply with applicable laws in their jurisdiction. Accounts may be restricted if suspicious, abusive, or prohibited activity is detected.

sections.heading: 5. Compliance and Prohibited Use
sections.text: CloudMine reserves the right to request identity information for AML/KYC purposes where required. Use of the platform for money laundering, sanctions evasion, fraud, unauthorized automation, or harmful interference is strictly prohibited.

sections.heading: 6. Limitation of Liability
sections.text: To the maximum extent permitted by law, CloudMine is not liable for indirect losses, lost profits, or damages caused by market volatility, blockchain events, third-party infrastructure failures, or user-side security incidents.

sections.heading: 7. Changes to Terms
sections.text: These terms may be updated periodically to reflect legal, technical, or operational changes. Continued use of the platform after updates constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
